w:r.w:t: FULL SOURCE CODE SECURITY AUDIT — 100 FINDINGS
Line-by-line review of 14,454 lines | 13 Solidity contracts + React frontend | March 2026

This audit identified 12 critical, 18 high, 30 medium, and 40 low severity findings across smart contracts, frontend code, and project architecture. The codebase shows security awareness (SECURITY FIX #N comments throughout), but several fixes are incomplete and the full source reveals issues invisible from the overview alone.

Top 6 critical chains: (1) votingPowerAt() ignores blockNumber — snapshot governance is theater. (2) TegridyPair charges 1% fee but UI shows 0.3% — real cost 1.3% + 5% default slippage = 6.3% per swap. (3) NFT position transfers orphan the first position permanently. (4) PremiumAccess.withdrawToTreasury drains the refund pool. (5) emergencyWithdrawPosition bypasses all lock restrictions with no access gate. (6) Zero tests across 14,454 lines of mainnet code.
votingPowerAt() ignores blockNumber — snapshot governance is fake

votingPowerAt(user, blockNumber) discards the blockNumber parameter and calls votingPowerOf(user) which returns current power. CommunityGrants.voteOnProposal() calls this at snapshotBlock, believing it gets historical power. Attacker buys TOWELI after proposal creation and votes with full weight.
Implement ERC20Votes checkpointing. Each stake/withdraw writes a checkpoint. votingPowerAt must binary-search checkpoint history.
NFT transfer overwrites userTokenId — positions become orphaned

_update() sets userTokenId[to]=tokenId unconditionally. If Alice owns tokenId=5 and buys tokenId=8 on OpenSea, her userTokenId becomes 8 and position 5 is invisible to pendingRewardOf(), votingPowerOf(), and all frontend hooks. Funds locked until expiry with no UI path to recover.
Revert in _update() if userTokenId[to]!=0, or convert to array, or remove 1-position-per-address constraint.

Transfers entire token balance to treasury including tokens held for cancelSubscription() refunds. Comment admits 'simplified approach.' Owner call → subscribers can't get pro-rata refunds. Directly defeats SECURITY FIX #17.
Track totalRefundableBalance. Only allow withdrawal of balance minus totalRefundableBalance.
SwapFeeRouter.swapExactETHForTokens — slippage checked on wrong amount

Fee deducted from ETH input, then reduced amount swapped with user's original amountOutMin. User expects X output for Y input, but fee reduces input to Y-fee while amountOutMin stays at X-worth. The tokensForETH path (FIX #5) correctly checks AFTER fee — this path was missed.
Pass 0 amountOutMin to router, check userReceives >= amountOutMin after receiving output (same pattern as tokensForETH).

K-invariant: balance*1000 - amountIn*10 encodes 1% fee (10/1000). Standard Uniswap V2 is 0.3% (3/1000). SwapFeeRouter adds 0.3% on top. SwapPage.tsx line 10619 shows fee:'0.3%'. Real total: 1.3%. Users pay 4.3x the disclosed fee.
Either reduce pair fee to 3/1000 or disclose real fee. Update SwapPage fee display to actual 1.3%.
SwapFeeRouter uses bare approve() — fails on USDT and non-standard tokens

IERC20(path[0]).approve(address(router), amountIn) is a raw call. USDT's approve() returns void, not bool — SafeERC20 isn't used here. Will revert for USDT swaps. Also: repeated approvals without resetting to 0 first fail on tokens requiring zero-first approval.
Use SafeERC20.forceApprove() which handles non-standard return values and resets allowance atomically.
Does not inherit ReentrancyGuard. swapExactTokensForETH sends ETH via .call{} at line 13719 before function completes. Malicious token or pair contract can re-enter.
Inherit ReentrancyGuard, apply nonReentrant to all external functions.
RevenueDistributor.claim() uses current lock for all historical epochs

Reads userLocked once at claim time, applies to ALL unclaimed epochs. User stakes 1 TOWELI at epoch 1, stakes 1M more at epoch 50, claims epochs 1-50 as if they had 1M the whole time. Massive overpayment on early epochs, theft from legitimate early stakers.
Snapshot per-user lock amounts per epoch, or require users to checkpoint before each distribution.
transferOwnership() is one-step. Wrong address = permanent loss of hook control. All other contracts use Ownable2Step for safety.
Use Ownable2Step pattern matching the rest of the codebase.
TegridyPair _mintFee sends 80% of fees to protocol, only 20% to LPs

numerator uses *4 making protocol take 4/5 (80%) of fee growth. Standard Uni V2 is 1/6 (16.7%). LPs earn 5x less than on Uniswap for same liquidity. Combined with 1% total fee: 0.8% goes to protocol, 0.2% to LPs. LP flight is guaranteed.
Reduce to standard 1/6 protocol share. At 1% total fee: 0.17% protocol, 0.83% LPs.
emergencyWithdrawPosition() bypasses lock with no access gate

Any user can call emergencyWithdrawPosition() at any time to withdraw staked tokens ignoring lockEnd. No paused-state requirement, no emergency flag. The entire lock mechanism (7d-4yr) is effectively optional — just forfeit rewards.
Gate behind an emergency flag that only owner can toggle, or remove entirely and rely on earlyWithdraw with 25% penalty.

useState(5) sets default slippage tolerance to 5%. Industry standard is 0.5%. Combined with hidden 1.3% fees, users lose up to 6.3% per swap. Preset buttons at line 10900 offer 1/3/5/10% — all too high. Slippage warning only triggers above 5% (line 10919).
Default to 0.5%. Warn at >1%. Show total cost (fee + slippage) prominently.
POLAccumulator sandwichable — swap+LP in one tx
accumulate() swaps half ETH for TOWELI then adds LP, atomically. Contract balance is public. Sandwich bots front-run the swap for guaranteed profit.
Use Flashbots Protect, or break into separate swap and LP steps with a time delay.
MemeBountyBoard submissions array — unbounded gas DOS

completeBounty() loops ALL submissions to find highest votes. An attacker submits thousands of empty entries (only gas cost) making completeBounty() exceed block gas limit. Bounty ETH permanently frozen.
Track topVoted submission ID on each voteForSubmission() call instead of scanning at completion.
CommunityGrants.executeProposal() — ETH stuck on recipient revert
If proposal.recipient is a contract that reverts on receive(), ETH stays in the contract but status is set to Executed. No retry or recovery mechanism.
Add try/catch. On failure, wrap ETH to WETH and push. Add admin function to reset failed executions.
JBAC boost cached forever after stake — ex-holders keep permanent bonus
hasJbacBoost cached at stake time, never re-validated. User stakes with JBAC NFT (+0.5x), sells NFT → keeps boost for entire lock duration. Free permanent boost extraction.
Accept as design decision and document, or add a keeper-callable revalidation function.
ReferralSplitter.recordFee() only callable by owner — bottleneck
onlyOwner modifier means only one EOA can record fees. If SwapFeeRouter needs to record fees, it can't call directly. Single point of failure.
Add approvedCallers mapping. Allow SwapFeeRouter to call recordFee() directly.
RevenueDistributor claim() gas DOS on epoch gaps
claim() loops from lastClaimedEpoch to current epoch. 1000 unclaimed epochs = 1000 iterations = block gas limit. claimUpTo() exists but main claim() has no cap.
Add MAX_CLAIM_EPOCHS constant (e.g., 100) to claim(). Revert with clear error directing to claimUpTo() if gap too large.
No Pausable pattern on any contract
Zero contracts implement OpenZeppelin Pausable. During an active exploit, there is no circuit breaker to halt staking/swapping/claiming while a fix is deployed.
Add Pausable to TegridyStaking, SwapFeeRouter, CommunityGrants, RevenueDistributor. Guardian role for emergency pauses.
CommunityGrants quorum uses totalLocked vs totalVotingPower
Quorum divides totalVotes (measured in votingPower = amount×boost) by totalLocked (raw amounts). 10% quorum can be met by 2.5% of lockers with max 4x boost. Inconsistent units.
Compute quorum against total votingPower, not totalLocked.
TegridyRestaking.claimAll() fails entirely if base claim reverts
Calls staking.claim(tokenId) which is external. If TegridyStaking is broken/paused, entire claimAll() reverts — user can't access bonus rewards either.
Wrap staking.claim() in try/catch. On failure, still distribute bonus rewards and emit an event.
MemeBountyBoard voting uses raw balanceOf — flash-loanable
voteForSubmission checks voteToken.balanceOf >= MIN_VOTE_BALANCE. Flash-loan 1000 TOWELI, vote on all submissions, return. Unlike CommunityGrants which uses votingPowerAt.
Use votingPowerOf() from TegridyStaking. Require staked position.
TegridyRestaking interface mismatch — missing hasJbacBoost in return
ITegridyStaking.positions() returns 7 values. Actual Position struct has 8 fields (hasJbacBoost added). Auto-generated getter returns all 8. Solidity ABI decoding may fail silently.
Update interface to match actual struct. Add integration test verifying cross-contract calls.
GeckoTerminal API price OVERRIDES on-chain price unconditionally
if(apiFallbackPrice>0) priceInUsd=apiFallbackPrice — overwrites on-chain calculated price. API error showing $1.00 when on-chain says $0.001 → all USD displays wrong (TVL, portfolio, IL calc).
Only use API if within ±30% of on-chain price. Flag discrepancies.
DCA feature is localStorage theater — no execution mechanism
Creates schedules in localStorage. dueSchedules computed but nothing calls executeSwap(). User must manually open app and click. Labeled as 'DCA' implying automation.
Add giant warning: 'Manual execution required'. Or integrate Chainlink Automation / Gelato.
Limit orders equally fake — vanish on tab close
Same as DCA. Pure localStorage. No keeper, no on-chain component. User sets limit, closes browser, price hits target — nothing happens.
Rename to 'Price Alerts' or implement real limit orders via keeper network.
Transaction receipt displays hardcoded 0.3% fee — actual is 1.3%
showReceipt({data:{fee:'0.3%'}}) hardcodes the fee display. User sees 0.3% in their shareable receipt, screenshot shows wrong fee. Material misrepresentation.
Calculate and display actual fee from contract feeBps + pair fee. Show total cost.
Suspense fallback={null} — blank screen during route loads
Lazy-loaded routes show nothing while JS chunks download. Combined with 2800ms transitions, app appears frozen for seconds.
Add skeleton loader or branded spinner as Suspense fallback.
useFarmActions toast fires inline — re-renders cause repeated calls
toast.success() in component body, not in useEffect. Runs on every render while isSuccess && hash is truthy. Only deduped by toast's id parameter — fragile.
Move to useEffect like useSwap.ts does.
ZERO test files — 14,454 lines deployed to mainnet untested
No Foundry tests, no Vitest, no Playwright. 13 contracts and full React frontend deployed to Ethereum mainnet handling real funds with zero automated test coverage.
Priority 1: Foundry test suite for all contracts. Priority 2: Vitest for hooks. Target 80%+ coverage before any further deployment.
emergencyWithdrawPosition vs earlyWithdraw — conflicting escape routes
earlyWithdraw takes 25% penalty. emergencyWithdrawPosition takes 0% penalty. Rational users always use emergency path, making the penalty mechanism useless.
Gate emergency behind paused state or remove it entirely.
Two identical functions, one marked DEPRECATED but still callable. Confusing for operators. Could accidentally use wrong one without realizing timelock applies to both.
Make setRewardPerSecond() revert with message directing to proposeRewardRate().
No minimum stake amount — dust position griefing
stake() only checks _amount==0. Stake 1 wei for 4 years → mint NFT, get voting power, get leaderboard position. Dust spam floods governance.
feeToSetter transferred in one step. Wrong address = permanent loss of fee configuration ability. Inconsistent with Ownable2Step used elsewhere.
TegridyPair uint112 reserves — overflow on high-supply tokens
_update() requires balance <= type(uint112).max (~5.19e33). For 18-decimal tokens: max ~5.19e15 tokens. Meme tokens with trillions of supply will brick the pair.
Document the limit. Add check in initialize() or use uint256 reserves.
Constructor approves TOWELI for type(uint256).max to router. If router is ever compromised or upgraded to a malicious version, all POL TOWELI is drainable.
Approve exact amounts per accumulate() call, or at least document the trust assumption.
Only checks deadline > block.timestamp. Bounty with deadline = block.timestamp + 1 second is valid. Too short for anyone to submit meaningful work.
CommunityGrants proposal fee non-refundable on rejection
42,069 TOWELI burned regardless of outcome. Spam deterrent but also punishes good-faith proposals that happen to fail. No partial refund mechanism.
Refund 50% on rejected proposals, 0% on cancelled, 100% burn on spam.
ReferralSplitter — referrer set permanently, no update mechanism
AlreadyReferred error if referrerOf[msg.sender] != address(0). Once set, cannot change. If referrer becomes malicious or inactive, no recourse.
Add updateReferrer() with a cooldown period (e.g., 30 days).
TegridyRouter _pairFor does lookup, not CREATE2 prediction
Uses factory.getPair() for every swap hop — external call per hop. Standard Uni V2 router uses CREATE2 address prediction (pure math, no external call). Gas overhead on every swap.
Compute pair address via CREATE2 prediction. Add INIT_CODE_HASH constant.
No event for TegridyFeeHook ownership transfer
transferOwnership() doesn't emit an event. Off-chain monitoring can't detect ownership changes.
RevenueDistributor.emergencyWithdraw can drain unclaimed user funds
When totalLocked==0, owner can withdraw ALL ETH including earmarked but unclaimed epoch rewards. Users who registered but haven't claimed lose their share.
Only withdraw balance minus totalEarmarked - totalClaimed (the excess).
PremiumAccess.cancelSubscription refund calculated on current fee rate

refundAmount = monthlyFeeToweli * remainingTime / MONTH. If owner raises monthlyFeeToweli, existing subscribers' refund increases (could exceed what they paid). If lowered, refund decreases below fair value.
Store the fee rate at subscription time and use that for refund calculation.
Gallery voting is trivially manipulable localStorage

Votes stored in localStorage per address. Clear storage → vote again. Script → thousands of votes. Different addresses → unlimited votes. If gallery rankings affect anything (bounties, commissions), this is exploitable.
Move voting on-chain with per-address dedup, or use signed messages verified server-side.
Points/Tegridy score is 100% client-side — trivially faked
Leaderboard rankings computed in browser from localStorage. DevTools → modify points → #1 on leaderboard. If scores gate access or airdrops, completely exploitable.
Compute scores server-side verifying against on-chain data (staking duration, tx count).
Custom token import trusts on-chain symbol/decimals without safety checks
handleImport() reads symbol and decimals from any address, creates TokenInfo, allows swapping. Honeypot tokens (fake USDC with transfer tax, or tokens that revert on sell) pass all checks.
Show contract age, holder count, liquidity info. Warn on low-liquidity or freshly deployed tokens. Check for known scam patterns.
QueryClient with zero config — no error handling, aggressive retries
new QueryClient() defaults: retry 3, no onError. Failed contract reads retry 3x silently. No global error handler for RPC failures.
Configure: retry:2, staleTime:30000, onError logging to monitoring.
No CSP or security headers in deployment
Only cache-control headers. No Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, HSTS, Permissions-Policy. DeFi app handling real funds with zero defense-in-depth.
Add full security header set in vercel.json.
GeckoTerminal iframe embedded without sandbox attribute

iframe src from geckoterminal.com with allow='clipboard-write' but no sandbox restriction. If GeckoTerminal is compromised, the iframe can navigate the parent, access same-origin storage, and inject scripts.
Add sandbox='allow-scripts allow-same-origin' to restrict iframe capabilities.
Referral from URL param auto-sets with no confirmation
Any ?ref=0x... parameter silently calls setReferrer(). A link like tegridy.app/swap?ref=0xAttacker permanently binds the user's referral. No confirmation, no ability to change later.
Show confirmation dialog: 'Set 0x... as your referrer?' before committing on-chain.
No gas estimation before any transaction
All writeContract calls fire directly to wallet with no pre-simulation. Users pay gas for reverted txs (expired deadlines, insufficient balance edge cases, contract paused).
Add simulateContract before writeContract. Show estimated gas cost in UI.
handleRestake only approves NFT — doesn't call restake()
Button says 'Approve & Restake NFT' but handleRestake() only calls approve(). User must make a second tx to call restake(). Two-step process disguised as one-click.
Chain the transactions: approve then restake, or use multicall. At minimum, change button text to 'Step 1: Approve NFT'.
Multiple components independently fetch GeckoTerminal API
Both hooks fetch the same endpoint. Two API calls per page load. localStorage writes race against each other.
Create single shared price context. Fetch once, share via React context.
slippageBps variable name is misleading — it's actually tenths
slippageBps = BigInt(Math.round(slippage * 10)); divided by 1000n. This is tenths, not basis points (which would be /10000). If anyone treats it as actual bps, slippage is off by 10x.
Rename to slippageTenths or use actual bps: slippage * 100 / 10000.
Slippage allows up to 20% — dangerously high max
handleSlippageChange allows num <= 20. At 20% slippage, sandwich bots extract 20% of every trade. No sane user needs >5% slippage.
Cap at 10% with explicit confirmation for anything above 3%.
localStorage quota not checked — silent data loss
30+ localStorage writes across the codebase. All wrapped in try{}catch{}. On quota overflow, all cached data (prices, DCA schedules, votes, points) silently stops persisting.
Add quota check utility. Implement LRU eviction for oldest entries.
Deploy script deploys all 8 contracts in one broadcast — no verification
Single vm.startBroadcast() deploys everything. One constructor failure = total deployment failure. No Etherscan verification between steps. No smoke testing.
Deploy individually with verification and testing between each contract.
No migration contract for existing stakers
Comment says 'users withdraw from old, deposit to new.' Multi-step manual migration with potential fund loss if old contract is broken or lock not expired.
Build atomic migration contract. Or implement batch migration callable by admin.
No monitoring, alerting, or error tracking
No Sentry, DataDog, Forta, or Defender. JS errors, failed txs, RPC failures, TVL drops — all invisible to the team.
Deploy Sentry for frontend. OpenZeppelin Defender for contract monitoring. Alert on TVL drops, admin calls, unusual patterns.
13 contracts deployed to mainnet — no audit by external firm
7 custom DeFi contracts + 3 AMM contracts + 3 peripheral contracts. No mention of Trail of Bits, OpenZeppelin, Spearbit, or any auditor. No bug bounty.
Commission professional audit. Launch Immunefi bug bounty. Run Slither + Mythril static analysis.
TegridyStaking.fund() is permissionless — anyone can fund rewards
No access control on fund(). Anyone can call it. Not a vulnerability per se, but could be used to manipulate APR displays by funding tiny amounts to trigger events.
Consider adding minimum funding amount or restricting to approved addresses.
Block.timestamp used for lock duration — minor miner manipulation
Miners can manipulate timestamp by ~15 seconds. Not severe for 7-day minimum locks but could affect edge cases near lockEnd boundaries.
Accept as known limitation. Don't use for second-level precision.
TegridyPair.initialize() callable by factory only — no re-initialization guard
Relies solely on factory check. If factory is compromised, pairs can be re-initialized with different tokens, stealing LP funds.
TegridyPair uses price0CumulativeLast/price1CumulativeLast naming from Uni V2 but doesn't implement TWAP
The pair tracks blockTimestampLast but never computes price accumulators. No TWAP oracle available for other contracts to use.
Implement Uniswap V2 TWAP accumulators if any contract needs manipulation-resistant pricing.
No EIP-2612 permit support on TOWELI or staking
Users must do separate approve() + action() transactions. No gasless approval via EIP-2612 permit, increasing friction and gas costs.
Add permit support to reduce transaction count.
TegridyStaking treasury variable is mutable with no timelock
setTreasury() changes treasury instantly with onlyOwner. If owner is compromised, attacker redirects all future penalty revenues.
Add timelock on treasury changes matching the reward rate timelock.
Fee can be changed instantly by owner up to MAX_FEE_BPS (1%). Users swapping in the same block as a fee increase pay more than expected.
Add 24h timelock and announcement event for fee changes.
Multiple contracts have setTreasury without timelock
All allow instant treasury address change. Common rug vector if owner key is compromised.
Add timelock to all treasury changes across all contracts.
CommunityGrants cancelProposal reuses ProposalNotActive error for auth check
Line: if (msg.sender != owner() && msg.sender != proposal.proposer) revert ProposalNotActive(). Uses wrong error name for access control failure.
MemeBountyBoard getBounty returns dummy uint256 parameter
Return signature includes 'uint256 dummy' that always returns 0. Dead return value wastes gas and creates confusing ABI.
Remove dummy parameter. Clean up return signature.
Google Fonts loaded without SRI integrity hashes
fonts.googleapis.com CSS and font files loaded without Subresource Integrity. Supply chain vector.
Self-host fonts or add SRI attributes.
Only one image preloaded — loader shows multiple art pieces
Only mfers-heaven.jpg preloaded. Loader cycles through 22 art pieces. First paint depends on download speed of whichever art is first.
React 19 used with no concurrent features
Latest React major version but only class-era patterns (no use(), no useTransition, no startTransition for route changes).
Leverage React 19 features for smoother transitions and data loading.
Price baseline resets every 24h — misleading % change display
priceChange calculated against session-based baseline, not real 24h change. Users may interpret as market data.
Label 'since session start' or compute real 24h change from historical API.
Confetti fires on every swap success including tiny amounts
confetti.fire() on every swap regardless of amount. Swapping $0.01 gets the same celebration as $10,000.
Threshold: only confetti for swaps above a meaningful amount.
No accessibility — canvas-heavy, dark-only, no keyboard nav
Heavy canvas animations, auto-playing audio, dark-only theme, no ARIA labels on interactive elements, no skip-to-content link.
Add ARIA labels. Respect prefers-reduced-motion. Add light theme. Keyboard navigation.
ErrorBoundary catches errors but doesn't report to monitoring
Errors displayed locally only. Team has zero visibility into production JS crashes.
Add Sentry or error reporting service integration.
useSwap eslint-disable-line on multiple useEffect deps
Multiple eslint-disable-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps — stale closure bugs are possible. Missing deps mean effects don't re-run when they should.
Fix dependency arrays properly instead of suppressing linter.
No debounce on vote button — rapid clicks stack votes/unvotes
No debounce on vote(). Rapid clicking toggles vote state faster than localStorage writes, potentially corrupting vote count.
Token logo onError hides image but doesn't show fallback
onError={e => (e.target as HTMLImageElement).style.display = 'none'} — broken logos just disappear. No placeholder icon.
Show symbol initial as fallback (already done in some places, inconsistent).
Aggregator comparison threshold too low at 0.5%
aggBetter triggers at >50bps. Could be stale data, not real improvement. Shows 'Better price found' for noise.
Increase to 1% minimum. Show both quotes side-by-side.
No network wrong-chain indicator on critical pages
useNetworkCheck exists but no evidence of prominent wrong-network warnings on Farm, Swap, or Restake pages.
Show full-page overlay or persistent banner when on wrong network.
Two production URLs with no canonical redirect
tegridyfarms.vercel.app and tegridyfarms-three.vercel.app both serve the app. SEO dilution, phishing confusion.
Redirect one to the other. Use custom domain as canonical.
Index.html no-cache but assets immutable — Safari still caches stale
Safari's aggressive caching can serve stale index.html despite no-cache. App might load old JS bundle pointing to wrong contract addresses.
Add Cache-Control: no-store for index.html specifically.
Loader audio autoplay will fail on all modern browsers
Web Audio API ambient loop blocked by autoplay policies. AudioContext starts suspended. Silent failures and console errors.
Only start audio after user gesture (the click-to-enter step). Handle suspended state.
ParticleBackground runs rAF loop continuously even when hidden
Canvas animation runs regardless of visibility. GPU waste when covered by modals or during transitions.
Pause on visibility change or IntersectionObserver.
2000 particles on desktop — battery drain on laptops
2000 particles with spring physics + WebGL2 post-processing. Mid-range laptops will drop frames and drain battery.
Detect hardware capabilities. Default to 500. Cap at 1000. Respect prefers-reduced-motion.
GlitchTransition 2800ms max duration — extremely slow navigation
Homepage transitions: 2800ms desktop, 2000ms mobile. Industry standard: 300ms. Navigating 5 pages wastes 14 seconds on pure animation.
Reduce to 300-500ms. Make skippable on any click.
Canvas noise overlay at 32ms refresh — pure GPU waste
Renders random noise canvas at ~31fps during transitions. A static noise PNG tile would achieve the same visual effect.
Use CSS background-image with tiled noise PNG.
backdrop-blur used extensively — jank on mobile scroll
Glass-card effects use backdrop-blur, one of the most expensive CSS properties. Mobile Safari has known performance issues.
Replace with semi-transparent solid backgrounds on mobile.
Foundry config has via_ir=true — slower compilation with no documented reason
via_ir increases compilation time significantly. Only needed for specific optimizer patterns. No documentation on why it's enabled.
Test with via_ir=false. Only enable if specific functions require it.
@metamask/sdk in dependencies but RainbowKit already handles MetaMask
Both @metamask/sdk and @rainbow-me/rainbowkit in dependencies. RainbowKit already supports MetaMask natively. Duplicate dependency adds bundle size.
Remove @metamask/sdk. Use RainbowKit's built-in MetaMask connector.
Environment variables referenced (VITE_WALLETCONNECT_PROJECT_ID, VITE_0X_API_KEY) but no .env.example file and no runtime validation that they exist.
Add .env.example. Validate required env vars at build time (Vite define plugin).
No pre-commit hooks or CI/CD pipeline
No husky, no lint-staged, no GitHub Actions. Developers can push untyped, unlinted code directly to main branch.
Add husky + lint-staged for pre-commit. GitHub Actions for CI (type check + lint + test).
react-is in dependencies but not used
react-is@19.2.4 in dependencies. Likely auto-added by a dependency. Unused direct dependency adds noise.
No README or documentation beyond overview
No README with setup instructions, no SECURITY.md, no CONTRIBUTING guide, no architecture decision records.
Deployment uses 'npx vercel --prod' — no versioned CI deploy
Manual CLI deployment from local machine. No version pinning, no build verification, no staging preview.
Use Vercel GitHub integration with preview deployments and production protection.
No lockfile or dependency pinning mentioned
Caret versions (^) throughout. npm install at different times produces different lockfiles. Supply chain attack surface.
Use exact versions or npm ci with committed lockfile.
clsx dependency for class concatenation — Tailwind already handles this
clsx imported but Tailwind CSS v4 with template literals handles conditional classes natively. Minor bundle bloat.
Evaluate if clsx is still needed. Remove if only used in a few places.
No Solidity NatSpec documentation on most functions
Some functions have ///@notice but many lack @param, @return, @dev documentation. Makes auditing and integration harder.
Add full NatSpec to all public/external functions.
